task.title: Searching the local file system
task.shortdesc: You can search your local file system for a file with a known name or pattern.

taskbody.prereq: local file systemsearchingIf you are going to work with local files in the Remote Systems view, you must first define a filter for the local file system. This filter might be mapped to the root of the system or to a specific location in the file system.

step.cmd: Select the local file system that you want to search by clicking the file system in the tree view.
Click + to expand Local and Local Files.
step.stepresult: The view lists filters for the local system.

step.cmd: Expand the appropriate filter to see your local folders and files.
Select a folder to search and then click Search on the menu.
step.stepresult: The Search window opens with the Folder populated. The cursor is positioned at the Search string (leave empty for file search) field.

step.cmd: If you want to specify a name pattern to search, enter the name pattern in the File name patterns field and select Search.
step.stepresult: If the search is successful, the results are displayed.

step.cmd: Look through the list of results and select the file that you want to see.

step.stepresult: The file opens in the editor.
